name: Qwen3.5-Plus
archDisplay: Hosted product tier of the Qwen3.5 hybrid-attention MoE line (Gated DeltaNet + Gated Attention + MoE, Qwen3-Next lineage); exact parameter/expert configuration for the specific hosted 'Plus' checkpoint undisclosed
note: Hosted/API counterpart to the open-weight Qwen3.5-397B-A17B, launched the same day via Alibaba Cloud Model Studio with a 1M-token default context, official built-in tools, and adaptive tool use on top of the open-weight base.

specSources.releaseDate: https://github.com/QwenLM/Qwen3.6 (README changelog, matches CNBC's Feb 17 coverage of the joint launch)

results.notes: Qwen3.5-Plus-NoThinking, used as the baseline column in the Qwen3.5-Omni launch blog's text-benchmark table
Qwen3.5-Plus-NoThinking column, Table 4 of the official Qwen3.5-Omni Technical Report (the column is used as the text-only baseline against Qwen3.5-Omni-Flash/Plus).
Qwen3.5-Plus-NoThinking column, Table 4 of the Qwen3.5-Omni Technical Report.
GPQA (Diamond), Qwen3.5-Plus-NoThinking column, Table 4 of the Qwen3.5-Omni Technical Report.
LiveCodeBench v6, Qwen3.5-Plus-NoThinking column, Table 4 of the Qwen3.5-Omni Technical Report.

Vals AI independent evaluation of Qwen3.5-Plus (Thinking mode), tested live via the Alibaba Cloud Model Studio API (vals.ai model page links directly to https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/en/model-studio/getting-started/models as the provider). Vals.ai's 'SWE-bench' tile is specifically SWE-bench Verified (confirmed via https://www.vals.ai/benchmarks listing: 'SWE-bench Verified, 69 models tested', matching the model page's '42/69' rank). Reported as 71.20% +/- 2.03 SEM.

Vals AI independent evaluation of Qwen3.5-Plus (Thinking mode). Labeled 'MMMU' on the model summary card but the underlying benchmark page (vals.ai/benchmarks/mmmu) is titled 'MMMU Pro' and follows the official MMMU-Pro protocol (~1,700 questions, 4-option, chain-of-thought) with 82 models tracked -- matches the model page's '82/82' (last place) ranking, confirming this is MMMU-Pro not base MMMU. This is a genuinely weak, unfavorable result (dead last of 82 models tested) and is included per the instruction to prioritize thoroughness over favorable-only results; it may reflect a harness/prompting mismatch specific to vals.ai's protocol rather than a true multimodal weakness, since Qwen3.5-Plus scores far higher on other official multimodal benchmarks (MMMU 85.0, MathVision 88.6 per vendor-side reporting for the sibling open-weight model) -- flagged for maintainer judgment rather than silently omitted.

verificationNotes:
Confirmed real via the official qwen.ai launch blog (which announces the open-weight 397B-A17B and the hosted Plus tier together) and the official Alibaba Cloud Model Studio pricing page, which lists a versioned snapshot 'qwen3.5-plus-2026-02-15' with explicit tiered pricing ($0.40/$2.40 per M for 0-256K, $0.50/$3.00 for 256K-1M, International) -- used the base tier. Independent corroboration: a third-party review (medium.com/@leucopsis, 'Qwen3.5-Plus Review') and CNBC's coverage of the joint launch both place the release at Feb 16-17, 2026, consistent with the official GitHub changelog date of 2026-02-16 (the pricing page's internal snapshot suffix of '2026-02-15' is a 1-day variance, likely a timezone/versioning artifact, common across this vendor's other Plus-tier entries in this corpus e.g. Qwen3.7-Plus). Benchmark results are only available as the 'NoThinking' baseline used in a later Qwen3.5-Omni comparison table (self-reported, single primary source) -- no independently-corroborated third-party score could be found in this pass; flagged as lower-confidence than the open-weight sibling's results. Architecture/params undisclosed for this hosted tier specifically, consistent with corpus convention for all Plus/Max hosted variants (Qwen3-Max, Qwen3.7-Max, Qwen3.7-Plus).

2026-07-16: Researched the six flagged core-benchmark gaps (mmlu, hle, math, aime, humaneval, mmmu) for Qwen3.5-Plus. Found and added a real score for mmmu (81.0, Qwen3.5-Plus-Instruct column, Table 6 "Vision -> Text performance of Qwen3.5-Omni and Qwen3.5-Plus-Instruct" of the Qwen3.5-Omni Technical Report, arxiv.org/abs/2604.15804) -- a base-MMMU result, distinct from the already-recorded independent mmmu-pro (22.77, vals.ai) entry for this model. For the other five gaps (mmlu, hle, math, aime, humaneval), checked: (a) the official qwen.ai/blog?id=qwen3.5 launch blog and the Qwen3.5-Omni Technical Report's Table 4 ("Text -> Text performance", Qwen3.5-Plus-Instruct column, fetched directly and transcribed in full) -- neither reports base MMLU, HLE, generic MATH, AIME, or HumanEval for the Plus tier; Table 4 instead reports MMLU-Pro/MMLU-Redux (already recorded), HMMT Nov 25/IMOAnswerBench for math/competition reasoning, and LiveCodeBench v6 (already recorded) for coding, in place of the legacy benchmarks; (b) the independent Vals AI model page (vals.ai/models/alibaba_qwen3.5-plus-thinking), whose complete 10-tile benchmark set for this model (CorpFin v2, MortgageTax, SAGE, Vibe Code Bench v1.1, GPQA Diamond, LiveCodeBench, LegalBench, MMLU Pro, MMMU[-Pro], SWE-bench) does not include AIME, MATH, HumanEval, HLE, or base MMLU; and (c) the sibling open-weight Qwen3.5-397B-A17B corpus entry, which does report hle=28.7 and aime=91.3 (AIME26) -- but those are self-reported scores for that separate open-weight checkpoint/model file, not the hosted Qwen3.5-Plus product, and are not safe to copy across models. Conclude all five are legitimate absences -- Alibaba has moved the Qwen3.5 generation's self-reported suite off legacy MMLU/MATH/HumanEval onto MMLU-Pro/MMLU-Redux, HMMT/IMOAnswerBench, and LiveCodeBench/SWE-bench respectively -- rather than an unresearched gap; omitted rather than guessed. Also note: several third-party SEO-aggregator pages (e.g. automatio.ai, digitalapplied.com) surfaced MMLU/MATH/HumanEval/AIME numbers attributed to "Qwen3.5-Plus" that do not match either the official launch blog or the Omni technical report (e.g. attributing the sibling 397B-A17B's GPQA score of 88.4 to "MMLU", and citing an unsourced "MATH: 74.1" / "HumanEval: 79.3" that appear nowhere in the primary sources) -- these were checked against primary sources and disregarded as unreliable/fabricated.

2026-07-18: Chased the flagged 'hle' (Humanity's Last Exam) gap for Qwen3.5-Plus specifically (the hosted Alibaba Cloud Model Studio product tier, distinct from the open-weight Qwen3.5-397B-A17B sibling checkpoint). Searched across 5 of the 6 prescribed methods:
(1) Official launch materials: re-checked qwen.ai/blog?id=qwen3.5 (JS-rendered, content not retrievable via fetch) and re-verified Table 4 ('Text -> Text performance of Qwen3.5-Omni and Qwen3.5-Plus-Instruct') of the Qwen3.5-Omni Technical Report (arxiv.org/abs/2604.15804 / arxiv.org/html/2604.15804) column-by-column -- confirmed columns are Knowledge (MMLU-Pro, MMLU-Redux, SuperGPQA, C-Eval), Instruction Following (IFEval, IFBench), Long Context (AA-LCR, LongBench v2), STEM (GPQA), Reasoning (LiveCodeBench v6, HMMT Nov 25, IMOAnswerBench), General Agent (BFCL-V4, TAU2Bench) -- no HLE column exists for the Plus-Instruct row.
(2) Independent trackers: Vals AI (vals.ai/models/alibaba_qwen3.5-plus-thinking) -- confirmed complete 10-tile set (CorpFin v2, MortgageTax, SAGE, Vibe Code Bench v1.1, GPQA Diamond, LiveCodeBench, LegalBench, MMLU Pro, MMMU, SWE-bench) has no HLE tile. llm-stats.com's own HLE leaderboard (llm-stats.com/benchmarks/humanity's-last-exam) was fetched and enumerated for every Qwen entry: Qwen3.5-27B (0.485), Qwen3.5-122B-A10B (0.475), Qwen3.5-35B-A3B (0.474), Qwen3.7 Max (0.414), Qwen3.7-Plus (0.347), Qwen3.6 Plus (0.288), Qwen3.5-397B-A17B (0.287), Qwen3.6-27B (0.240) -- 'Qwen3.5-Plus' (the hosted tier under review) does not appear at all; only its open-weight sibling 397B-A17B is tracked. Artificial Analysis has no page at artificialanalysis.ai/models/qwen3-5-plus (404) -- only a separate 'Qwen3.5 Omni Plus' model page exists, which is a different (omni/multimodal) product. BenchLM's Qwen3.5-Plus profile page shows only 4 of 316 tracked benchmarks (JobBench, Vibe Code Bench v1.1, FrontierMath v2 Tiers 1-3, FrontierMath v2 Tier 4) -- no HLE. Scale SEAL's HLE (Text Only) leaderboard (labs.scale.com/leaderboard/humanitys_last_exam_text_only) was enumerated for Qwen entries: only Qwen3-235B-A22B-Thinking-2507 (15.43) and Qwen3-235B-A22B (11.75) appear -- no Qwen3.5-Plus entry. Epoch AI's HLE benchmark page (epoch.ai/benchmarks/hle) has no queryable leaderboard content in the fetched excerpt and no Qwen3.5-Plus-specific data found via search.
(3) Academic sources: the arXiv paper (method 1 above) is also the primary academic source; no other arXiv/Semantic Scholar paper found citing Qwen3.5-Plus's HLE score as a baseline.
(4) GitHub eval-harness logs: checked github.com/QwenLM/Qwen3.6 README -- benchmark results are presented only as linked images (not machine-readable table text) and the README explicitly defers to the Qwen3.5 blog for details; no HLE figure surfaced.
(5) Sibling-model comparison tables: checked three independent Qwen3.6-Plus-vs-Qwen3.5-Plus comparison writeups (aimadetools.com/blog/qwen-3-6-vs-3-5, designforonline.com/ai-models/qwen-qwen3-6-plus, tesseraai.cloud/en/blog/qwen-3-6-vs-3-5-performance-comparison) -- none report an HLE number attributed to Qwen3.5-Plus (one reports Qwen3.6-Plus's own HLE at 25.7%, which is a different, newer model and not usable here); a fourth comparison (qubrid.com, codersera.com/blog/qwen-3-5-complete-guide-2026) likewise had no HLE figure for Qwen3.5-Plus.
Note: the sibling open-weight checkpoint Qwen3.5-397B-A17B does have a well-corroborated HLE score of 28.7 (Hugging Face model card, cross-confirmed by llm-stats.com's leaderboard entry at 0.287) -- but per the established corpus convention already documented in this file's prior verification pass, that is a distinct model/checkpoint from the hosted Qwen3.5-Plus product and is not copied across. Conclusion: HLE is a genuine, unresolved absence for the hosted Qwen3.5-Plus tier specifically -- not yet independently benchmarked or self-reported for this exact SKU -- rather than an unresearched gap. Omitted rather than guessed or borrowed from the sibling.
